days.title: City places and the question где?

grammar.title: Asking where: где? and the idea of location
grammar.explanation:
To ask **where** something is located, use **где?**. To answer, you can simply point with **здесь** (here) or **там** (there): _Где парк? — Парк там._

This week we learn how to say that something or someone is located *in* or *at* a place. Russian uses a special form of the noun for this — the **prepositional case** (also called the locative). It always appears after the prepositions **в** (in) and **на** (on/at). We start the case endings tomorrow; today just get comfortable naming city places and asking где?

examples.english: Where is the park? — The park is here.

reading.title: Где магазин?
reading.text: — Где магазин? — Магазин там. — А парк? — Парк здесь.
reading.translation: — Where is the shop? — The shop is there. — And the park? — The park is here.

days.speakingPrompt: Point around an imaginary map of your town and say where three places are. Use где?, здесь and там, e.g. "Где школа? Школа там."

days.title: The в + prepositional ending -е

vocab.exampleEn: I am in the park now.

grammar.title: Saying "in" a place: в + prepositional -е
grammar.explanation:
To say something is located **in** a place, use **в** plus the **prepositional case**. For most masculine and feminine nouns, you add **-е** to the noun.

Masculine nouns ending in a consonant just add **-е**: банк → в банк**е**, парк → в парк**е**, магазин → в магазин**е**. Feminine nouns ending in **-а** change it to **-е**: школа → в школ**е**, аптека → в аптек**е**.

Note: **дома** is a fixed adverb meaning *at home* — it is not в + case.

reading.title: Где мама?
reading.text: — Где мама? — Мама в магазине. — А папа? — Папа дома.
reading.translation: — Where is Mum? — Mum is in the shop. — And Dad? — Dad is at home.

days.speakingPrompt: Say where four people are right now using в + prepositional, e.g. "Я в парке. Мама в банке. Папа дома." Include at least one город place from today and one from day 1.

days.title: The на + prepositional: work, places with на

vocab.exampleEn: Mum is at the post office.
I am at the train station.

grammar.title: Saying "at" a place: на + prepositional
grammar.explanation:
Some places take **на** instead of **в**, but the ending is the same prepositional **-е**.

Use **на** with: **работа** → на работ**е** (at work), **почта** → на почт**е**, **вокзал** → на вокзал**е**, **рынок** → на рынк**е** (note the о drops out: рынок → рынке), **стадион** → на стадион**е**.

There is no easy rule for which places take в and which take на — you simply learn them. Most buildings (банк, магазин, школа) use **в**; open spaces and many events/activities use **на**.

examples.english: I am at the post office.
Brother is at the train station.

reading.title: Папа на работе
reading.text: — Где папа? — Папа на работе. — А ты? — Я дома, а брат на стадионе.
reading.translation: — Where is Dad? — Dad is at work. — And you? — I am at home, and my brother is at the stadium.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe where five family members are, using both в and на correctly, e.g. "Папа на работе, мама в магазине, брат на стадионе."

days.title: Special endings: -ии, -ь nouns, and в/на choices

vocab.exampleEn: The children are outside (on the street).
I am in the park too.

grammar.title: A few tricky forms: unchanging nouns and -й endings
grammar.explanation:
Most nouns take **-е** in the prepositional, but watch for these:

**Indeclinable nouns** never change: **кафе** stays the same — _в кафе_ (in the café). Borrowed words ending in **-о/-е** like кафе, метро do not change.

Nouns ending in **-й** replace it with **-е**: **музей** → в музе**е**.

Feminine nouns in **-а** still give **-е**: квартира → в квартир**е**, улица → на улиц**е**. Note **улица** takes **на** (на улице = outside / on the street).

Use **тоже** to say *also*: _Я в парке, и мама тоже в парке._

reading.title: В кафе
reading.text: — Где ты? — Я в кафе. — А мама? — Мама тоже в кафе, а дети на улице.
reading.translation: — Where are you? — I am in the café. — And Mum? — Mum is also in the café, and the children are outside.

days.speakingPrompt: Say where you and two other people are, using at least one indeclinable noun (кафе) and тоже, e.g. "Я в кафе. Брат тоже в кафе. Мама в музее."

days.title: Putting it together: где ты? в городе vs дома

grammar.title: Locating in the city: в городе, в центре
grammar.explanation:
Now combine everything. You can locate yourself broadly (**в городе** — in the city, **в центре** — downtown) or precisely (**в офисе**, **в больнице**).

Remember the pattern: **в/на + noun ending in -е**.
- Masculine: офис → в офис**е**, центр → в центр**е**.
- Feminine -а: больница → в больниц**е**, гостиница → в гостиниц**е**.

Add distance words to describe places: **далеко** (far) and **близко** (near). _Банк далеко, а аптека близко._

examples.english: The school is near, but the station is far.

reading.title: Мы в центре
reading.text: — Где вы? — Мы в центре, в кафе. — Это близко? — Нет, это далеко.
reading.translation: — Where are you (pl.)? — We are downtown, in a café. — Is it close? — No, it is far.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe a small map of your city: name where you and your family are, and say which places are близко and which are далеко. Use в/на + prepositional throughout.

days.title: Talking about your day: где работа, где живёшь

vocab.example: Я живу и работаю в городе.
vocab.exampleEn: I live and work in the city.

grammar.title: Using location with живу and работаю
grammar.explanation:
The prepositional is used after verbs of being and living/working **somewhere**. The location ending stays the same **-е** pattern you have learned.

**жить** (to live): я **живу**, он/она **живёт**. **работать** (to work): я **работаю**, он/она **работает**.

Put them with в/на + place: _Я живу в городе. Я работаю в банке. Папа работает в офисе._

Join two ideas with **и** (and): _Я живу и работаю в центре._

examples.russian: Я живу в квартире и работаю в офисе.
examples.english: I live in an apartment and work in an office.

reading.title: Где ты живёшь?
reading.text: — Где ты живёшь? — Я живу в центре, в квартире. — А где работаешь? — Я работаю в банке.
reading.translation: — Where do you live? — I live downtown, in an apartment. — And where do you work? — I work at the bank.

days.speakingPrompt: Introduce yourself: say where you live and where you work, then say where one family member lives and works. Use живу/живёт and работаю/работает with в/на + prepositional.

days.title: Review: places in the city and в/на + где

grammar.title: Recap: prepositional case for location
grammar.explanation:
This week you learned to say **where** people and things are located.

**Key pattern:** **в** or **на** + noun with the prepositional ending, usually **-е**.
- Masculine consonant: банк → в банк**е**, офис → в офис**е**.
- Masculine -й: музей → в музе**е**.
- Feminine -а: школа → в школ**е**, больница → в больниц**е**.
- Indeclinable: кафе stays **кафе**.

**в vs на:** buildings usually take **в** (в магазине, в школе); work, open spaces and some places take **на** (на работе, на улице, на вокзале). **дома** = at home (fixed form).

Ask with **где?** and answer with **здесь / там / близко / далеко** or a в/на phrase.

examples.english: Where is Mum? — Mum is in the shop.

examples.russian: Папа на работе, а я дома.
examples.english: Dad is at work, and I am at home.

examples.russian: Я живу в городе и работаю в банке.
examples.english: I live in the city and work at the bank.

reading.title: В городе
reading.text: — Где ты сейчас? — Я в центре, в кафе. — А мама? — Мама на работе, а папа дома. — Это далеко? — Нет, близко.
reading.translation: — Where are you now? — I am downtown, in a café. — And Mum? — Mum is at work, and Dad is at home. — Is it far? — No, close.

days.speakingPrompt: Have a short imaginary phone conversation: ask and answer where three different people are right now. Use где?, в/на + prepositional, дома, близко and далеко. Then say one sentence about where you live and work.
